title: Exclusive tests should not be commited to version control
description: Exclusive tests should not be commited to version controlAnalyze your codeintentionality-completereliabilityBugQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEWhy is this an issue?More InfoWhen using testing frameworks like Mocha and Jest, appending.only()to the test function allows running a single test case for a file. Using.only()means no other test from this file is executed. This is useful when debugging a specific use case.describe("MyClass", function () { it.only("should run correctly", function () { // Noncompliant /*...*/ }); });However, it should not be used in production or development, as it is likely a leftover from debugging and serves no purpose in those contexts.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Exclusive tests (.only) should not be committed to version control
